lean4_sketch:
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Gamma.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.Summation.Summable
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

-- Placeholder for Gaussian hypergeometric function
-- This function is not yet defined in Mathlib4.
-- See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypergeometric_function
-- _2F1(a,b;c;z) = ∑ (n=0 to ∞) (a)ₙ(b)ₙ / (c)ₙ * zⁿ/n!
namespace Real
noncomputable def Hypergeometric._2F1 (a b c z : ℝ) : ℝ := sorry
end Real

-- Theorem: bernstein_constant (v16)

theorem bernstein_constant :
    ∑' (n : ℕ), (Nat.choose (2 * n) n : ℝ) / (4^n * (4 * n + 1)) =
    (Real.Gamma (1 / 4) * Real.Gamma (1 / 2)) / (4 * Real.Gamma (3 / 4)) := by
  -- The proof proceeds by identifying the sum as a case of the Gaussian hypergeometric function
  -- _2F1(a,b;c;z) at z=1, applying Gauss's theorem for its evaluation, and simplifying
  -- the resulting expression involving the Gamma function.

  -- Sub-Lemma 1 — 🟢 EASY: Prove positivity / non-vanishing of the special function's values.
  -- This ensures the denominator of the right-hand side is non-zero.
  have h_pos_denom_nonzero : 4 * Real.Gamma (3 / 4) ≠ 0 := by
    have h_gamma_pos : 0 < Real.Gamma (3 / 4) := by
      apply Real.Gamma_pos_of_pos
      norm_num
    exact mul_ne_zero (by norm_num) (ne_of_gt h_gamma_pos)

  -- Sub-Lemma 2 — 🔴 HARD: Establish the absolute summability of the series.
  -- This is required for the sum to be well-defined.
  have h_conv : Summable (λ n : ℕ, (Nat.choose (2 * n) n : ℝ) / (4^n * (4 * n + 1))) := by
    -- The proof of convergence relies on the asymptotic behavior of the central binomial coefficient,
    -- showing the terms are O(n⁻³/²). Since 3/2 > 1, the series converges by comparison
    -- with a p-series. This is a non-trivial result from asymptotics.
    sorry

  -- Sub-Lemma 3 — 🟡 MEDIUM: Apply the functional equation for the Gamma function.
  -- This is key to simplifying the expression from Gauss's theorem.
  have h_rec : Real.Gamma (5 / 4) = (1 / 4) * Real.Gamma (1 / 4) := b

import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Gamma.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.Summation.Summable
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic

-- Placeholder for Gaussian hypergeometric function
-- This function is not yet defined in Mathlib4.
-- See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypergeometric_function
-- _2F1(a,b;c;z) = ∑ (n=0 to ∞) (a)ₙ(b)ₙ / (c)ₙ * zⁿ/n!
namespace Real
noncomputable def Hypergeometric._2F1 (a b c z : ℝ) : ℝ := sorry
end Real

-- Theorem: bernstein_constant (v16)

theorem bernstein_constant :
    ∑' (n : ℕ), (Nat.choose (2 * n) n : ℝ) / (4^n * (4 * n + 1)) =
    (Real.Gamma (1 / 4) * Real.Gamma (1 / 2)) / (4 * Real.Gamma (3 / 4)) := by
  -- The proof proceeds by identifying the sum as a case of the Gaussian hypergeometric function
  -- _2F1(a,b;c;z) at z=1, applying Gauss's theorem for its evaluation, and simplifying
  -- the resulting expression involving the Gamma function.

  -- Sub-Lemma 1 — 🟢 EASY: Prove positivity / non-vanishing of the special function's values.
  -- This ensures the denominator of the right-hand side is non-zero.
  have h_pos_denom_nonzero : 4 * Real.Gamma (3 / 4) ≠ 0 := by
    have h_gamma_pos : 0 < Real.Gamma (3 / 4) := by
      apply Real.Gamma_pos_of_pos
      norm_num
    exact mul_ne_zero (by norm_num) (ne_of_gt h_gamma_pos)

  -- Sub-Lemma 2 — 🔴 HARD: Establish the absolute summability of the series.
  -- This is required for the sum to be well-defined.
  have h_conv : Summable (λ n : ℕ, (Nat.choose (2 * n) n : ℝ) / (4^n * (4 * n + 1))) := by
    -- The proof of convergence relies on the asymptotic behavior of the central binomial coefficient,
    -- showing the terms are O(n⁻³/²). Since 3/2 > 1, the series converges by comparison
    -- with a p-series. This is a non-trivial result from asymptotics.
    sorry

  -- Sub-Lemma 3 — 🟡 MEDIUM: Apply the functional equation for the Gamma function.
  -- This is key to simplifying the expression from Gauss's theorem.
  have h_rec : Real.Gamma (5 / 4) = (1 / 4) * Real.Gamma (1 / 4) := b
